methodology: Compiled from maltainfoguide.com, sceneongozo.com and maltavents.com's 2026 guides, then substantially expanded via an island-wide deep-dive (four parallel regional research passes) cross-checking every entry against the Archdiocese of Malta's official 'Festi f'Malta u f'Għawdex' calendar (knisja.mt / church.mt) - the single most authoritative source for exact festa dates - plus targeted searches (parish/band-club sites, tourism listings, local news, social media) to verify which feasts have real mortali/aerial fireworks evidence rather than just a procession or band march. The app now covers festas year-round rather than just the traditional April-October season, so confirmed winter feasts are included too. 'major' = titular/parish festa; 'secondary' = a real, non-titular (often confraternity) feast that still involves fireworks. Confidence: 'high' = specific, feast-level fireworks evidence from 2+ sources or a well-documented dedicated fireworks society; 'medium' = a real, confirmed feast with plausible-but-not-independently-verified fireworks; 'low' = feast confirmed to exist but fireworks evidence is thin, generic, or contested - treat these as 'worth knowing about' rather than 'expect a big display.' Times are still not officially published in most cases; see the app's general mortali-timing pattern instead of per-festa times.
flaggedForReview: Marsa - Holy Trinity: sources disagree between 14 June 2026 and 31 May 2026; we've gone with 14 June pending confirmation against the parish's own 2026 programme.
Ħamrun - Immaculate Conception (a second Ħamrun feast, alongside the existing San Ġejtanu one): exact 2026 date is unconfirmed, estimated ~5 July from a stale source; verify closer to the date.
Birkirkara - St Anthony (Sant'Antnin): sources disagree between 17 and 18 May 2026; held at a chapel rather than the main parish church, likely a smaller devotional feast.
Sliema - St Francis of Assisi: sources disagree between 6 and 12 October 2026; included at low confidence pending clarification.
Għasri, Gozo: the October feast's exact date is now also disputed (11 vs 13 Oct) on top of its already-uncertain fireworks scale; confidence downgraded to low.
Victoria (Rabat, Gozo) - Our Lady of Graces: one source gives 20 September instead of the 13 September used here; day-of-week reasoning favours 13 September but worth a final check.
Balluta (on the St Julian's/Sliema border) doesn't have its own locality entry, so its Our Lady of Mount Carmel feast is filed under 'st-julians'.
Xagħra, Gozo 'Jesus of Nazareth' (27 Sep) - a real, smaller secondary feast with a documented fireworks tradition dating to 1924, genuinely quieter than the village's main 8 Sep festa.
Gwardamanġa/Pietà - Our Lady of Fatima: Pietà has no parish of its own and shares Gwardamanġa's; the date is a best estimate (~first week of June) and fireworks scale is unconfirmed.

Deliberately excluded despite existing evidence of a real feast: Sliema's Lenten 'Jesus of Nazareth' procession (27 Mar - solemn, no fireworks tradition), Mellieħa's St Joseph (3 May) and Our Lady of the Rosary (4 Oct), Għaxaq's St Joseph (7 Jun), Mqabba's Immaculate Conception (8 Dec), Żebbuġ (Malta)'s St Joseph (26 Jul), and Kerċem's discontinued 'St Gregory the Great' observance (external procession stopped in 1968) - none had fireworks evidence.
Confirmed to have NO festa of their own (verified absence, not a gap): Pembroke, Pietà, Swieqi (nearest parish is Tal-Ibraġ, 8 Dec, also with no fireworks evidence), Xgħajra, Mdina, and Mtarfa.

festas.title: Holy Family (Il-Familja Mqaddsa)
festas.notes: Iklin's only parish feast; a winter date with no evidence of significant fireworks. Included for completeness now that the app covers the full year.

festas.title: St Paul's Shipwreck
festas.notes: Valletta's own titular feast, with a confirmed aerial fireworks display over Grand Harbour. Winter festa, included now the app covers the full year.

festas.title: St Joseph (Ta' Ġieżu)
festas.notes: Confraternity feast at the Ta' Ġieżu church; fireworks are fired from the L'Isle Adam Band Club house as the statue returns.

festas.title: St Joseph the Worker (San Ġużepp Ħaddiem)
festas.notes: Own parish within Birkirkara, distinct from the main August festas; described as having ground and aerial fireworks.

festas.title: Holy Cross (Salib Imqaddes)
festas.notes: Held at the Sant'Elena church within Birkirkara; described as having ground and aerial fireworks.

festas.title: St George Preca
festas.notes: Malta's newest parish, dedicated to a saint canonised in 2007; no dedicated fireworks factory found, likely modest at most.

festas.title: St Anthony (Sant'Antnin)
festas.notes: Held at a chapel rather than the main parish church; sources disagree on the exact date (17 vs 18 May) and no fireworks evidence was found.

festas.title: St Anthony of Padua
festas.notes: Second Għajnsielem feast alongside Our Lady of Loreto; procession includes a traditional bomb salute, aerial mortali not independently confirmed.

festas.title: St Paul Shipwreck
festas.notes: Munxar's sole titular festa; documented fireworks tradition, though modest/small-scale relative to Malta's biggest villages. Distinct from the separate, secular 'Fireworks & Gastronomy Festival' held in Munxar in late June.

festas.title: Our Lady of Fatima
festas.notes: Shared parish with Pietà. Date is a best estimate; fireworks scale unconfirmed.

festas.title: The Annunciation of Mary
festas.notes: Date moved from the traditional 31 May to 7 June 2026 due to the general election.

festas.title: Holy Trinity (Trinità Qaddisa)
festas.notes: See flaggedForReview: sources disagree between 14 June and 31 May 2026. Confirmed real fireworks tradition via the 'Għaqda Festi Esterni SSma Trinità Marsa' association.

festas.title: The Visitation of Our Lady
festas.notes: Corrected from an earlier '22 June, Our Lady of Ta' Pinu' entry - that date is actually a separate pilgrimage/apparition anniversary at the Ta' Pinu sanctuary with no fireworks tradition. This is Għarb's real titular fireworks festa.

festas.title: Our Lady of the Sacred Heart (Sacro Cuor)
festas.notes: Confirmed air and land fireworks; the feast that started this whole deep-dive - previously missing from the dataset entirely.

festas.title: Our Lady of Mount Carmel
festas.notes: Fleur-de-Lys is administratively part of Birkirkara but has its own parish (est. 1975); Carmel feasts are near-universally mortali-heavy in Malta, but not independently confirmed for this specific parish.

festas.title: Immaculate Conception (Immakulata Kunċizzjoni)
festas.notes: Ħamrun's second parish, alongside San Ġejtanu. Fireworks confirmed via historical parish video evidence, but the exact 2026 date is unresolved - see flaggedForReview.

festas.title: Madonna tad-Duttrina
festas.notes: Curia-approved permanent date to avoid clashing with the Annunziata festa; organisers on record preparing both land and air fireworks displays.

festas.title: Our Lady of Mount Carmel (Balluta)
festas.notes: Balluta sits on the St Julian's/Sliema border and has no locality entry of its own; heavy street decoration confirmed, aerial fireworks plausible but not independently confirmed.

festas.title: Marija Addolorata (Our Lady of Sorrows)
festas.notes: The historic titular parish feast of the old town of St Paul's Bay itself, distinct from the newer Qawra St Francis parish also in this locality.

festas.title: St Peter in Chains
festas.notes: Known for ground fireworks displays, typically on the Saturday evening.

festas.title: St Dominic (San Duminku)
festas.notes: Confraternity feast at the Dominican church; confirmed ground and aerial fireworks displays.

festas.title: Christ Our Saviour
festas.notes: Renowned for exceptionally elaborate aerial fireworks displays.

festas.title: Santa Marija (Assumption)
festas.notes: One of Malta's grandest fireworks displays.

festas.title: Santa Marija (Assumption)
festas.notes: Birkirkara has two large August festas 8 days apart with St Helen; both confirmed as real, separate, full-scale festas.

festas.title: St Helen
festas.notes: Birkirkara's principal parish festa, one of Malta's largest.

festas.title: Our Lady Star of the Sea (Stella Maris)
Santa Marija (Assumption)
festas.notes: Corrected from 15 to 23 August - Żebbuġ's Assumption feast belongs to a separate parish/band club from the 14 June St Philip feast and follows its own date, confirmed against the Archdiocese's parish-verified 2026 calendar.

festas.title: Our Lady of Lourdes
festas.notes: Paola's second parish (est. 1974), with its own dedicated fireworks & decorations association.

festas.title: St Dominic (San Duminku)
festas.notes: Part of Malta's UNESCO-recognised festa tradition, but sources describe this feast as built on elaborate street decoration rather than fireworks specifically.

festas.title: Our Lady of Consolation (Madonna taċ-Ċintura)
festas.notes: St Augustine's parish confraternity feast; no Valletta-specific fireworks evidence found.

festas.title: St Gregory the Great (San Girgor)
festas.notes: Confirmed feast-specific aerial fireworks, described as the more laid-back of Sliema's feasts - smaller than Stella Maris but real.

festas.title: Our Lady of Graces
festas.notes: See flaggedForReview: one source gives 20 September instead.

festas.title: St Augustine (Santu Wistin)
festas.notes: Same parish as Our Lady of Consolation; some evidence of a confetti-type 'sciuta' tradition, but also reports of the external festa being cancelled in at least one recent year.

festas.title: St Leonard (San Leonardu)
festas.notes: Kirkop's second parish feast alongside St Joseph; confirmed synchronised fireworks display evidence.

festas.title: Jesus of Nazareth
festas.notes: A real, smaller secondary feast with a documented fireworks tradition dating to 1924 - genuinely quieter than Xagħra's main Sep 8 festa.

festas.title: Il-Madonna tar-Rużarju (Our Lady of the Rosary)
festas.notes: Gudja's own La Stella Fireworks Factory (the society behind the August festa) confirmed to fire a display for this occasion too, though scale relative to the main festa is unconfirmed.

festas.title: St Francis of Assisi
festas.notes: Secondary Sacro Cuor parish feast; sources disagree between 6 and 12 October, and no feast-specific fireworks evidence was found.

festas.title: Our Lady of Patronage
festas.notes: Għasri is one of Gozo's smallest villages; fireworks scale unconfirmed and the exact date is now also disputed (11 vs 13 Oct).

festas.title: St John of the Cross (San Ġwann tas-Salib)
festas.notes: Ta' Xbiex's own parish feast, run by the Discalced Carmelites. Late-autumn date with no fireworks or band-club evidence found; likely modest or none. Included for completeness now the app covers the full year.

festas.title: Immaculate Conception (Kunċizzjoni)
festas.notes: Bormla's main titular feast; eyewitness accounts describe a substantial synchronised aerial fireworks show. Winter festa, included now the app covers the full year.
